title: Deliciously Braised Argentinian Chuck Roast Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew featuring tender chuck roast, infused with the brightness of yuzu and perfect for a flexitarian brunch, this dish is a fresh take on traditional Argentinian cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chuck roast cubes with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Sear the chuck roast cubes until browned on all sides, then remove from the pot.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it starts to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped red bell pepper and cook until it starts to soften.
Add the yuzu juice, canned diced tomatoes, and beef broth to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned chuck roast cubes to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
